root.narration: Your ball is on the green, just 6 feet from the hole. You pull out your putter -- the flat club used for rolling the ball on the green. You want to make this putt! How do you hit it?

choices.text: Use a smooth, gentle back-and-forth motion like a pendulum, keeping your eyes on the ball
Take a big swing at the ball to make sure it gets to the hole
Hit the ball with a quick jab and look up right away to watch it

outcome.headline: It Drops! Nice Putt!
outcome.explanation: Your smooth stroke rolls the ball right into the center of the cup. A putting stroke is like a pendulum on a clock -- smooth back, smooth forward. Keeping your eyes on the ball helps you hit it in the sweet spot of the putter.
outcome.whatToRemember: Putting is all about a smooth, steady stroke. Back and through, like a pendulum. Keep your head still and your eyes on the ball.

keyTerms.definition: The flat-faced club used on the green to roll the ball into the hole.
A smooth back-and-forth motion, like a clock pendulum. This is how you swing a putter.
The short, smooth grass around the hole where you use your putter.

outcome.headline: Way Past the Hole!
outcome.explanation: You hit the ball way too hard and it rolls past the hole by 10 feet. Now you have a longer putt coming back. On the green, you only need a small, gentle stroke. The ball rolls easily on the short grass.
outcome.whatToRemember: Putting does not need a big swing. The green is smooth and the ball rolls easily. Use a small, gentle stroke to control how far the ball goes.

outcome.headline: You Missed! Head Came Up Too Soon
outcome.explanation: By jabbing at the ball and looking up right away, you hit the ball off the toe of the putter. It squirts off to the right and misses the hole by three feet. When you look up too early, your whole body moves and the putter face twists.
outcome.whatToRemember: Keep your head down and your eyes on the ball until after you have hit it. Looking up too soon is the number one putting mistake.
